title: Schedule HVAC shutdown at 6 PM in Lecture Hall Complex
description: Analysis shows Lecture Hall Complex HVAC system continues running 2-3 hours after closing time. Implementing an automated shutdown schedule at 6:00 PM could significantly reduce energy waste without impacting occupant comfort.

title: Install motion-sensor lighting in Lecture Hall Complex corridors
description: Corridor lights in Lecture Hall Complex remain on 24/7. Installing PIR motion sensors could reduce lighting energy by 40% during low-occupancy hours, particularly overnight and weekends.

title: Implement waste segregation drive in Lecture Hall Complex
description: Lecture Hall Complex has a waste diversion rate below 35%. A targeted waste segregation awareness campaign and installing color-coded bins could improve recyclable recovery by 25%.

title: Upgrade to LED lighting in Lecture Hall Complex
description: Approximately 60% of lighting fixtures in Lecture Hall Complex use fluorescent tubes. Replacement with LED panels would reduce lighting energy consumption by 50% with improved lumen output.

title: Optimize Lecture Hall Complex server room cooling
description: The server room in Lecture Hall Complex is over-cooled, maintaining 18°C when 24°C is sufficient per ASHRAE guidelines. Raising the setpoint would reduce cooling energy by 15%.

title: Install solar panels on Lecture Hall Complex rooftop
description: Lecture Hall Complex has 18000 sqft of unshaded rooftop space ideal for a 50kW solar installation. This could offset 30% of the building's daytime energy consumption.

title: Reduce standby power in Lecture Hall Complex labs
description: Lab equipment in Lecture Hall Complex draws significant standby power overnight. Installing smart power strips with scheduled shutoffs could reduce phantom load by 60%.
